tool_input.summary: To quantify how energetic Mercury’s ring current is during active times, you typically need (1) magnetic-field perturbations to infer current strength/energy content and (2) in-situ plasma/energetic-particle measurements to compute pressure/energy density. The most relevant PDS holdings are MESSENGER MAG (field) plus MESSENGER EPPS (FIPS thermal plasma and EPS energetic particles). PDS4 bundles exist for these, with legacy PDS3 volumes available for the same products.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Primary calibrated Mercury magnetometer time series used to identify active intervals and derive ring-current signatures (ΔB, current systems) during disturbances.

candidates.title: MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Residuals Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Provides MAG residuals after subtracting the KT17 internal field model, helping isolate external/magnetospheric current contributions relevant to ring-current energy estimates.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Derived plasma products from FIPS (energy/charge <50 eV/q to 20 keV/q) useful for computing ion pressure/energy density associated with ring/current-sheet populations during active times.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS EPS DDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Derived energetic particle products from EPS (electrons ~25 to >500 keV; ions ~10 keV/nuc to ~3 MeV) to quantify the energetic particle contribution to ring-current energy content during active periods.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS Calibrated FIPS Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated FIPS observations supporting custom calculation of distribution functions, moments, and energy density for ring-current-related ions.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS Calibrated EPS Calibrated Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated EPS observations supporting event-by-event energetic particle spectra needed to compute energy content during active intervals.

candidates.title: MESS E V H SW MAG 3 CDR CALIBRATED V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 calibrated MAG volume (same measurement basis as the PDS4 MAG calibrated bundle) for Mercury magnetospheric activity/ring-current analyses.

candidates.title: MESS E V H SW MAG 4 SUMM CALIBRATED V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 summarized calibrated MAG products useful for surveying active intervals and correlating with ring-current enhancements.

candidates.title: MESS E V H SW EPPS 3 FIPS DDR V2.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 derived FIPS products (plasma) for computing energy density/pressure during ring-current-active times.

candidates.title: MESS E V H SW EPPS 3 EPS DDR V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 derived EPS products (energetic particles) for quantifying energetic particle energy content associated with ring-current enhancements.

dataset.description: This bundle contains MESSENGER Energetic Particle and Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) advanced data products, also known as DDR/DAPs. This bundle contains data from the Energetic Particle Spectrometer (EPS) subsystem. EPS covers the energy range of 25 to > 500 keV for electrons, and 10 keV/nucleon to ~3 MeV total energy for ions. dataset.description: This bundle contains MESSENGER Energetic Particle and Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) calibrated observations, also known as CDRs. This bundle contains Fast Imaging Plasma Spectrometer (FIPS) instrument data. FIPS covers the energy/charge range of less than 50 eV/q to 20 keV/q. parts.args: {"summary":"To quantify how energetic Mercury’s ring current is during active times, you typically need (1) magnetic-field perturbations to infer current strength/energy content and (2) in-situ plasma/energetic-particle measurements to compute pressure/energy density. The most relevant PDS holdings are MESSENGER MAG (field) plus MESSENGER EPPS (FIPS thermal plasma and EPS energetic particles). PDS4 bundles exist for these, with legacy PDS3 volumes available for the same products.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-mag-calibrated::1.0","title":"MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data
